subject: Geology
title: Specific Gravity
description: A quick, practical guide to Specific Gravity.

hook.text: Pick up galena and it feels shockingly heavy. Heft alone can help identify a mineral if you know what to feel.

buildup.text: Specific gravity compares a mineral's density to water. Quartz is about 2.7; galena is 7.6; gold is 19.3.

discovery.text: Archimedes reportedly used water displacement to detect a fake gold crown. The same principle identifies minerals.

twist.text: In the field, geologists often just heft a sample. Experience turns an imprecise guess into a reliable clue.

climax.text: Specific gravity separates minerals that look alike. Two silver-colored minerals can have very different weights.

punchline.text: Weight reveals what the eyes cannot.

quiz.question: What does specific gravity compare?
options: A mineral's color to its streak
